name: MS-QRD-005: Lateral Movement via SMB — Rapid Multi-Host Access
description: Detects lateral movement activity where a single internal host authenticates to multiple other internal hosts via SMB (port 445) or NetBIOS (port 139) within a short time window. Attackers use SMB for lateral movement via techniques such as PsExec, WMI, DCOM, or direct share access after obtaining valid credentials through credential dumping. Rapid authentication to 3+ distinct internal destinations within 5 minutes is a strong indicator of automated lateral movement.
author: MilliSec Blue Team — Rihad Ebilov

qradar.aql: SELECT sourceip AS "Source IP (Pivot Host)", username AS "Account Used", LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS "Log Source", COUNT(DISTINCT destinationip) AS "Unique Target Hosts", COUNT(*) AS "Total SMB Events", MIN(DATEFORMAT(starttime, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')) AS "First Seen", MAX(DATEFORMAT(starttime, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')) AS "Last Seen", SUM(CASE WHEN CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%success%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "Successful Auths", SUM(CASE WHEN CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%fail%' OR CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%denied%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "Failed Auths" FROM events WHERE (destinationport IN (445, 139) OR CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%smb%' OR CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%admin share%' OR CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%file share%') AND sourceip IS NOT NULL AND destinationip IS NOT NULL AND sourceip != destinationip AND (INCIDR('10.0.0.0/8', sourceip) OR INCIDR('172.16.0.0/12', sourceip) OR INCIDR('192.168.0.0/16', sourceip)) AND (INCIDR('10.0.0.0/8', destinationip) OR INCIDR('172.16.0.0/12', destinationip) OR INCIDR('192.168.0.0/16', destinationip)) GROUP BY sourceip, username, LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT destinationip) >= 3 ORDER BY "Unique Target Hosts" DESC LAST 5 MINUTES
qradar.offense_relevance: Single source authenticating to 3+ internal SMB targets within 5 minutes is a high-confidence lateral movement indicator. Especially suspicious when the account used is a standard user (not a domain admin service account). The 'Successful Auths' vs 'Failed Auths' ratio reveals whether the attacker had valid credentials (high success ratio = Pass-the-Hash or stolen creds) or is attempting brute force (high fail ratio). Correlate with EventID 4624 LogonType=3 from Splunk rule MS-SPL-009.
response_actions: Block source IP from internal SMB access at firewall ACL
Reset password for the username shown in alert immediately
Investigate all destination hosts accessed — check for newly created services, scheduled tasks, or files
Run forensic timeline on source host to identify attack entry point
Check for PsExec artifacts: PSEXESVC service, admin$ share access in Event 5140
Invalidate all Kerberos tickets for the affected account (klist purge on DC)
qradar.false_positive_notes: Domain controllers performing replication (SYSVOL/NETLOGON share access). Backup agents (Veeam, Backup Exec) accessing multiple hosts simultaneously. Software deployment tools (SCCM, PDQ Deploy) pushing updates. IT admin performing legitimate multi-host maintenance. Whitelist known backup and deployment server IPs.
qradar.tuning_notes: Threshold COUNT(DISTINCT destinationip) >= 3 within 5 minutes is aggressive — adjust to 5 if environment has high legitimate SMB activity. For domain controllers and backup servers, add AND sourceip NOT IN (SELECT value FROM REFERENCETABLE('DC_and_Backup_IPs')) to WHERE clause. Consider splitting into two rules: one for workstation-sourced SMB (lower threshold=3) and one for server-sourced SMB (higher threshold=10).
